name: Beall's List (archived)
notes: Original predatory publishers list by librarian Jeffrey Beall; taken down 2017; archived versions available

name: Stop Predatory Journals (GitHub)
notes: Community-maintained GitHub repo with publishers.csv and journals.csv; CC0 licensed

name: FTC vs OMICS International
notes: US Federal Trade Commission charged OMICS with deceptive practices; $50.1M judgment 2019

name: Caltech Questionable Conferences (Dana Roth)
notes: Curated by Caltech science librarian Dana Roth; one of the most comprehensive conference-specific lists

name: Dolos List of Dubious Conferences
notes: 34 dubious organizers; blog maintained by researchers

name: boytchev/spam (GitHub)
notes: Academic spam metadata collected by researcher Pavel Boytchev; 100+ conference organizers documented

name: Community Submissions
notes: Researcher-submitted entries via GitHub issues; require evidence URL before acceptance

name: Think. Check. Attend.
notes: Not a list but a checklist tool; referenced for criteria definition

name: Wikipedia: Predatory Conference
notes: Reference article with documented cases; not a primary source but useful for evidence URLs

name: PreDefense Paper (arXiv)
notes: Academic paper documenting predatory AI/ML conferences; good source for CS-specific entries
